name: spooftooph
title: spooftooph

commands.name: spooftooph

arguments.description: Number of Bluetooth profiles to display per page
arguments.raw: -b <num_lines>	: Number of Bluetooth profiles to display per page

arguments.description: Disable banner for smaller screens (like phones)
arguments.raw: -B 		: Disable banner for smaller screens (like phones)

arguments.description: Assign random NAME, CLASS, and ADDR
arguments.raw: -R		: Assign random NAME, CLASS, and ADDR

arguments.description: Scan for devices in local area
arguments.raw: -s		: Scan for devices in local area

arguments.description: Time interval to clone device in range
arguments.raw: -t <time>	: Time interval to clone device in range

arguments.description: USB delay.  Interactive delay for reinitializing interface
arguments.raw: -u		: USB delay.  Interactive delay for reinitializing interface

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# spooftooph -h
 
 spooftooph v0.5.2 by JP Dunning (.ronin) 
 <www.hackfromacave.com>
 (c) 2009-2012 Shadow Cave LLC.
 
 NAME
 	spooftooph
 
 SYNOPSIS
 	spooftooph -i dev [-mstu] [-nac]|[-R]|[-r file] [-w file]
 
 DESCRIPTION
 	-a <address>	: Specify new BD_ADDR
 	-b <num_lines>	: Number of Bluetooth profiles to display per page
 	-B 		: Disable banner for smaller screens (like phones)
 	-c <class>	: Specify new CLASS
 	-h		: Help
 	-i <dev>	: Specify interface
 	-m		: Specify multiple interfaces during selection
 	-n <name>	: Specify new NAME
 	-r <file>	: Read in CSV logfile
 	-R		: Assign random NAME, CLASS, and ADDR
 	-s		: Scan for devices in local area
 	-t <time>	: Time interval to clone device in range
 	-u		: USB delay.  Interactive delay for reinitializing interface
 	-w <file>	: Write to CSV logfile
 			  (Useful in Virtualized environment when USB must be passed through.)

examples.command:
spooftooph -i hci1 -a 00803789EE76
Manufacturer:   Broadcom Corporation (15)
Device address: 00:19:0E:0E:EA:4B

binary_metadata.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB pie executable, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked, interpreter /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2, BuildID[sha1]=6af0d2c807d478cd2abdeb0ecd0d2c292e377514, for GNU/Linux 3.2.0, stripped

apt_metadata.description:
Automates spoofing or cloning Bluetooth devices
Spooftooph is designed to automate spoofing or cloning
Bluetooth device Name, Class, and Address. Cloning this
information effectively allows Bluetooth device to hide in
plain site. Bluetooth scanning software will only list one
of the devices if more than one device in range shares the
same device information when the devices are in Discoverable
Mode (specificaly the same Address).

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use spooftooph because ### spooftooph.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair spooftooph with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `spooftooph --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### spooftooph.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install spooftooph
